key_insight: Exciton transport in perovskite nanocrystal solids is governed more by energetic disorder than by structural polydispersity, and longer alkylamine ligands can reduce transport efficiency despite improving monodispersity.
engineering_relevance: Ligand length is a practical design knob for tuning nanocrystal solid optoelectronic performance in LEDs, lasers, and solar cells.
method: Resolved exciton dynamics in space, time, and energy.
Compared disorder effects across nanocrystal solids synthesized with different alkylamine ligand lengths.
Linked structural/energetic disorder to transport efficiency.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that shorter ligands produce more polydisperse particles, while longer ligands yield more monodisperse but more energetically disordered nanocrystals, and the latter show less efficient exciton transport.
watch_item: Improving size uniformity alone may not improve device performance if quantum-confinement-driven energetic disorder remains high.
paper_title: Exciton Transport in Disordered Perovskite Nanocrystal Solids
